summary: Corrected the signedness of StageInfo.internal_stage_id in src/melee/gr/stage.h, changing the field at offset 0x88 from u32 to s32. The PR was a minimal stage header type fix with no review discussion in the provided slice.
what_changed: In typedef struct _StageInfo, the field internal_stage_id at offset 0x88 was changed from u32 to s32.
No other files or symbols changed in the provided diff.
smart_moves: Kept the fix narrowly scoped to the header declaration, minimizing risk while correcting the type information used by stage code.
Preserved the field name, offset comment, and surrounding struct layout while only changing signedness, which helps avoid unnecessary churn in decompilation headers.
decomp_lessons: For struct fields representing IDs, signedness can matter even when size and layout are unchanged; matching work should preserve the semantic type expected by comparisons, sentinel values, or casts.
Header-only type corrections are valuable in decomp projects because they improve downstream function signatures and decompiled expressions without altering binary layout.
When fixing a struct field type, retain offset comments and existing layout documentation to make the mechanical nature of the correction clear.
naming_conventions: StageInfo uses the field name internal_stage_id for the stage identifier at offset 0x88.
The project style in this header uses fixed-width aliases such as u32 and s32 for struct fields.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changing u32 to s32 keeps the field width at 32 bits, so the struct layout remains unchanged while allowing signed operations or signed sentinel handling to be represented accurately.

This kind of signedness correction may help decompiled C better match instructions involving signed comparisons, sign-aware casts, or negative constants, though the provided PR slice does not show the specific motivating function.
follow_up_queries: Find uses of StageInfo.internal_stage_id that rely on signed comparisons or negative sentinel values.
Audit other stage identifier fields for signedness consistency with internal_stage_id.
Check whether decompiled stage code produces better matches when internal_stage_id is treated as s32.
